text.title: Personalization, Exit Intent, and Retargeting
text.summary: Segment-based personalization lifts (15-40%), exit-intent recovery patterns, and retargeting channel timing and conversion benchmarks.

section.title: Personalization by Segment

rule.statement: Personalize messaging by segment before running A/B tests on a single message — segment lift (15-40%) typically exceeds copy optimization lift (5-15%).
rule.rationale: A generic message optimized for the average user underperforms a segment-specific message for each audience.

list.item: Traffic source: match ad message to landing page — lift 15-30%
Geographic: local language, currency, examples — lift 10-20%
Device: mobile-optimized vs desktop layout — lift 10-25%
Returning users: skip intro, show new features — lift 20-40%
User intent: different CTAs per goal — lift 15-35%

section.reference: Worksheet for prioritizing personalization hypotheses by expected lift

section.title: Exit Intent

rule.statement: Trigger exit intent only when user has not already converted and has not dismissed the overlay in this session.
rule.rationale: Showing exit intent to converted users or repeat dismissers is noise that damages trust.

section.p: Realistic recovery rate: 5-15% of abandoning users. Offer must have clear value (discount, free trial, consultation).
section.reference: Exit intent configuration template with exclusion and tracking hooks

section.title: Retargeting Channels

list.item: Email immediate (within 24h): remind of value, no hard sell — recovery 5-10%
Email 24-48h: social proof + small incentive — recovery 3-8%
Ad retargeting 1-7 days: social proof creative + offer — recovery 2-5%

section.reference: 3-email retargeting sequence with timing, goals, and metric targets

example.note: Stripe-style sequence: Hour 0 — "Questions about Stripe?" (15% open, 3% conversion); Day 1 — case study (20% open, 5% conversion); Day 3 — "Start in 10 minutes" + $500 credit (18% open, 8% conversion). Overall 12% recovery rate.

antipattern.description: Sending identical messaging to all retargeting segments regardless of where they dropped off.
antipattern.reason: A cart abandoner needs a different message than a pricing-page abandoner. Irrelevant retargeting increases unsubscribe rate and wastes ad budget.
